actions.value:
In order to send a token on Binance Smart Chain (BEP-20), for example USDT, USDC or DAI, you'll first need a bit of BNB to pay gas fee. For any EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine), it is such that tokens can not pay their own fees. Only the network's primary coin, in this case, BNB, is capable of paying these fees.

 

This is a EVM-blockchain policy, not an Edge one. We receive no profit from these gas fees, and they are paid solely to BSC-network block validators to process your transaction on-chain.

 

This policy also applies to exchanges, so if you want to swap a BEP20 token into anything else (for example USDT to BNB), you'll first need some BNB in your BSC wallet to cover said fees. Transactions on Binance Smart Chain are fairly inexpensive, so even $1 of BNB is typically enough to process 4-10 transactions.

 

Please let us know if you still have any questions.
Token is stuck in Binance Smart Chain wallet with a 0 BNB balance
